title: NIPSCERN Has 5 Papers Accepted at INSCIT 2026

pt.title: NIPSCERN tem 5 artigos aceitos no INSCIT 2026
pt.excerpt: O 10º Simpósio Internacional de Sistemas de Instrumentação, Circuitos e Transdutores aceitou cinco trabalhos do grupo NIPSCERN, abrangendo síntese de alto nível em FPGA para o trigger do ATLAS, reconstrução de energia por deconvolução com pseudoinversa, cancelamento polo-zero com filtro de Wiener, conformador digital IIR em FPGA e simulação contínua de sinais com o framework Lorenzetti.
pt.content:
O NIPSCERN tem o prazer de anunciar que cinco artigos do grupo foram aceitos para apresentação no 10º Simpósio Internacional de Sistemas de Instrumentação, Circuitos e Transdutores (INSCIT 2026), que ocorrerá no evento CHIP in Sampa, de 24 a 28 de agosto de 2026, em São Paulo, SP.

Parabéns, Melissa S. Aguiar e Dabson F. Santos! O trabalho de vocês sobre o redesign do firmware do back-end do trigger do Calorímetro de Argônio Líquido do ATLAS por Síntese de Alto Nível (HLS) foi aceito. Em parceria com Marcos V. S. Oliveira (BNL) e Luciano M. A. Filho, o artigo apresenta uma nova arquitetura de matriz de chaveamento paralelo no bloco REMAP, reduzindo a utilização da FPGA de 69% para 64% e habilitando ciclos de desenvolvimento mais rápidos e exploração sistemática do espaço de projeto sem alteração do código RTL legado.

Parabéns, Melissa S. Aguiar, Eder B. Kapisch e Ualison R. F. Dias! O artigo de vocês sobre reconstrução de energia online por técnica de deconvolução baseada na pseudoinversa de Moore-Penrose foi aceito. Desenvolvido em parceria com Huacheng Cai e Denis O. Damazio (CERN/BNL) e Luciano M. A. Filho, o método reduz o erro de reconstrução em até 62,5% em comparação com o Filtro Ótimo convencional e apresenta recuperação natural de sinais impulsivos, com arquitetura adequada para implementação em FPGA em sistemas de trigger em tempo real.

Parabéns, Leandro da G. Ribeiro, Thiago C. A. Paschoalin e Tiago M. Quirino! O trabalho de vocês sobre cancelamento polo-zero combinado com filtragem de Wiener para estabilização de linha de base em condições de alta taxa de eventos foi aceito. Supervisionado por Luciano M. A. Filho e desenvolvido em colaboração com Ualison R. F. Dias, o estudo demonstra redução do RMSE de aproximadamente 120 para 9,5 counts ADC em cenários de alta ocupação — resultado relevante para sistemas de calorimetria em física de altas energias.

Parabéns, Fábio C. Luna! Sua implementação digital de um estágio de condicionamento de sinal em FPGA para simulação de pulsos em instrumentação nuclear foi aceita. Realizado em colaboração com Thiago C. A. Paschoalin, Tiago M. Quirino e Luciano M. A. Filho, o trabalho aplica o Método das Invariantes ao Impulso para discretizar a função de transferência analógica do conformador de cauda longa, decompondo-a em seções paralelas de primeira e segunda ordem com aritmética de ponto fixo — reproduzindo o comportamento esperado com menos de 1% dos recursos da placa Xilinx KCU105.

Parabéns, Nelson Cevidanes N. de Assis e Tiago Aparecido Teixeira! O artigo de vocês sobre simulação contínua de fluxo de sinais de front-end em instrumentação nuclear usando o framework Lorenzetti foi aceito. Em colaboração com Guilherme Barroso Morett, Gustavo Barbosa Libotte, Bernardo Sotto Maior Peralva (UERJ) e Luciano M. A. Filho, o trabalho reinterpreta dados de Minimum Bias como um fluxo contínuo de colisões modelado por processo de Poisson, viabilizando o estudo de algoritmos de trigger de primeiro nível em fluxos de dados realistas e ininterruptos — funcionalidade ainda não disponível em simuladores orientados a eventos.

Parabenizamos todos os autores, colaboradores e co-orientadores envolvidos neste conjunto notável de contribuições. A diversidade desses trabalhos — do firmware de trigger do ATLAS a ferramentas abertas de simulação — reflete o compromisso contínuo do NIPSCERN com o avanço da instrumentação, do processamento de sinais e dos sistemas de tempo real para física de altas energias.

excerpt: The 10th International Symposium on Instrumentation Systems, Circuits, and Transducers (INSCIT 2026) accepted five papers from the NIPSCERN group, spanning HLS-based FPGA firmware for the ATLAS LAr trigger, deconvolutional energy reconstruction via pseudoinverse, pole-zero cancellation with Wiener filtering, an IIR digital shaper on FPGA, and continuous signal simulation using the Lorenzetti framework.
content:
NIPSCERN is proud to announce that five papers from our group have been accepted for presentation at the 10th International Symposium on Instrumentation Systems, Circuits, and Transducers (INSCIT 2026), taking place at CHIP in Sampa, August 24–28, 2026, in São Paulo, SP.

Congratulations, Melissa S. Aguiar and Dabson F. Santos! Your work on the High-Level Synthesis-based firmware redesign of the ATLAS Liquid Argon Calorimeter digital trigger back-end has been accepted. Co-authored with Marcos V. S. Oliveira (BNL) and Luciano M. A. Filho, the paper introduces a parallel switch matrix architecture for the REMAP block, reducing FPGA utilisation from 69% to 64% while enabling faster development cycles and systematic design-space exploration without modifying the legacy RTL code.

Congratulations, Melissa S. Aguiar, Eder B. Kapisch, and Ualison R. F. Dias! Your paper on a deconvolutional online energy reconstruction technique based on the Moore-Penrose pseudoinverse has been accepted. Developed in collaboration with Huacheng Cai and Denis O. Damazio (CERN/BNL) and Luciano M. A. Filho, the method reduces pile-up reconstruction error by up to 62.5% compared to the conventional Optimal Filter and is naturally suited for low-latency FPGA implementation in real-time trigger systems.

Congratulations, Leandro da G. Ribeiro, Thiago C. A. Paschoalin, and Tiago M. Quirino! Your investigation of Pole-Zero Cancellation combined with Wiener filtering for baseline stabilisation under high event-rate conditions has been accepted. Supervised by Luciano M. A. Filho and carried out in collaboration with Ualison R. F. Dias, the work demonstrates RMSE reductions from approximately 120 to 9.5 ADC counts in high-occupancy scenarios — a significant improvement for calorimetry systems in high-energy physics.

Congratulations, Fábio C. Luna! Your digital implementation of a signal conditioning stage on FPGA for pulse simulation in nuclear instrumentation has been accepted. In collaboration with Thiago C. A. Paschoalin, Tiago M. Quirino, and Luciano M. A. Filho, the design applies the Impulse Invariant Method to discretise the analogue transfer function and decomposes it into parallel first- and second-order sections with fixed-point arithmetic, faithfully reproducing the long-tail shaper response using less than 1% of the resources on the Xilinx KCU105 board.

Congratulations, Nelson Cevidanes N. de Assis and Tiago Aparecido Teixeira! Your work on continuous front-end signal flow simulation in nuclear instrumentation using the Lorenzetti framework has been accepted. In collaboration with Guilherme Barroso Morett, Gustavo Barbosa Libotte, Bernardo Sotto Maior Peralva (UERJ), and Luciano M. A. Filho, the paper reinterprets Minimum Bias data as a free-running collision stream driven by a Poisson model, enabling the study of first-level trigger algorithms on realistic, uninterrupted data streams — a capability not previously available in event-driven simulators.

We extend our congratulations to all authors, collaborators, and co-supervisors involved in this remarkable set of contributions. The breadth of these works — from ATLAS trigger firmware deployed at CERN to open-framework simulation tools — exemplifies the depth and diversity of research carried out at NIPSCERN.

title: CGVWeb on the ATLAS Control Room Screen — A Milestone in History

pt.title: CGVWeb na Tela da Sala de Controle do ATLAS — Um Marco na História
pt.excerpt: Cinco meses. Dois fusos horários. Duzentas mil células de calorímetro reconstruídas em um navegador. Após testes bem-sucedidos na Run 3, o CGVWeb foi aprovado para ser o display oficial de eventos do ATLAS a partir da Run 4.

excerpt: Five months. Two clocks. Two hundred thousand calorimeter cells rebuilt in a browser. After successful Run 3 testing, CGVWeb has been approved to be the official ATLAS event display once Run 4 begins.
content: Five months after a graduate-school class at UFJF planted the idea, CGVWeb is running on the main wall of the ATLAS Control Room at CERN. After Run 3 tests that succeeded on every front, the LAr group, the calorimeter coordinators, and the ATLAS executive board approved CGVWeb as the official ATLAS calorimeter event display for Run 4. Built by Prof. Dr. Luciano Manhães de Andrade Filho and the NIPSCERN undergraduate Chrysthofer Arthur Amaro Afonso — with the LAr layer measurements provided by Mateus A. Carvalho and Roberta L. Leonardo — the tool reconstructs roughly 200,000 calorimeter cells, cell by cell, at 60+ fps in a browser tab, using Three.js, WebGPU, Rust and WebAssembly. Read the full feature for the complete story, from the first WebGL2 prototype to the deployment inside Point 1.

title: ATLAS Collaboration Awarded 2025 Breakthrough Prize in Fundamental Physics
excerpt: The ATLAS Collaboration at CERN has been awarded the 2025 Breakthrough Prize in Fundamental Physics — the world's largest science prize. NIPSCERN's own Luciano Manhães de Andrade Filho is among the honoured members.
content:
The Breakthrough Prize Foundation has awarded the 2025 Breakthrough Prize in Fundamental Physics to the ATLAS Collaboration at CERN. Widely regarded as the most valuable prize in science, the Breakthrough Prize recognises transformative contributions to fundamental research.

The ATLAS Collaboration — comprising more than 5,900 physicists from 181 institutions across 42 countries — was recognised for decades of discoveries at the Large Hadron Collider, including the 2012 discovery of the Higgs boson, precision measurements of the Standard Model, and searches for physics beyond it.

We are profoundly proud to announce that NIPSCERN's Dr. Luciano Manhães de Andrade Filho, coordinator of our laboratory and a long-standing ATLAS member, is among the collaboration members sharing this honour. His contributions span calorimeter instrumentation, the TileCal data readout system, and the development of event display tools — including the CaloGeoView and CGV-WEB software that allow physicists worldwide to visualise ATLAS collision data.

This recognition affirms the global impact of the collaborative effort to which NIPSCERN has contributed for over a decade through its partnerships with CERN, FAPEMIG, CNPq, CAPES, and UFJF.

The Breakthrough Prize ceremony takes place in November 2025 at the NASA Ames Research Center in Silicon Valley.

title: Chrysthofer Wins Prize at SEMIC 2025 for Fractal Video on FPGA via SAPHO
excerpt: NIPSCERN student Chrysthofer received a prize at SEMIC 2025 for his video article on generating the Mandelbrot set in real time on an FPGA, programmed using NIPSCERN's SAPHO toolchain. The video surpassed 4,000 views, 1,000 likes, and 500 comments.
content:
We are delighted to celebrate another achievement from within the NIPSCERN community. Chrysthofer, an undergraduate student at UFJF affiliated with NIPSCERN, received a distinguished prize at SEMIC 2025 — Seminário de Computação, hosted at ENMC (Encontro Nacional de Modelagem Computacional).

His awarded work presents a fully functional implementation of the Mandelbrot set fractal on an FPGA, rendered in real time without a CPU. The programming was performed entirely using NIPSCERN's SAPHO toolchain — demonstrating in a striking way the practical reach of our hardware architecture and compiler ecosystem.

The accompanying video showcases the vivid fractal geometry emerging on screen as the FPGA computes each pixel row by row, driven by CMM programs compiled with YANC and loaded onto hardware synthesised through the SAPHO environment. The video resonated deeply with the scientific computing and FPGA communities, accumulating over 4,000 views, more than 1,000 likes, and 500 comments.

This work sits at the intersection of scientific computing, hardware design, and mathematical visualisation — three pillars of research at NIPSCERN. We congratulate Chrysthofer on this well-deserved recognition and look forward to seeing more of his work.

Note: NIPSCERN's Dr. Luciano Manhães de Andrade Filho was also recently awarded the 2025 Breakthrough Prize in Fundamental Physics as a member of the ATLAS Collaboration at CERN — a year of exceptional recognition for the laboratory.

awards.title: Prize at SEMIC 2025 — Fractal Generation on FPGA

title: NIPSCERN Members Approved to INCT CERN-Brasil
excerpt: Luciano Manhães de Andrade Filho, Chrysthofer Arthur Amaro Afonso, and other NIPSCERN collaborators were approved as members of the INCT CERN-Brasil — Brazil's National Institute of Science and Technology for High Energy Physics at CERN.
content:
We are proud to announce a major milestone for NIPSCERN: in May 2025, our laboratory coordinator Prof. Dr. Luciano Manhães de Andrade Filho and undergraduate researcher Chrysthofer Arthur Amaro Afonso, along with other collaborators from our group, were formally approved as members of the INCT CERN-Brasil.

The INCT CERN-Brasil — Instituto Nacional de Ciência e Tecnologia para Física de Altas Energias com o CERN — is a Brazilian National Institute of Science and Technology established to coordinate and strengthen Brazil's scientific collaboration with CERN, the European Organization for Nuclear Research in Geneva, Switzerland. The INCT was approved by CNPQ in December 2022 and received its first funding in June 2023, bringing together researchers from across Brazil who contribute to CERN experiments including ATLAS, CMS, ALICE, and LHCb.

Membership in the INCT CERN-Brasil represents formal recognition of NIPSCERN's sustained scientific contributions to high energy physics, calorimeter instrumentation, and the ATLAS TileCal detector. It also strengthens our connections with the broader Brazilian physics community and opens new avenues for collaborative research and student exchange between UFJF and partner institutions.

This approval reinforces NIPSCERN's position as a leading Brazilian laboratory in the field of experimental particle physics and scientific computing, and underscores the impact of more than a decade of research at the frontier of modern science.
